ddls:
CREATE TABLE "demographic_data" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic data entry */,
  "year" INTEGER /* The specific year of the demographic data */,
  "value" REAL /* The demographic value for the specific year */,
  "source" TEXT /* The source of the demographic data */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data was added to the system */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data was last updated */,
  "population_type_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the population type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographic_data_population_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("population_type_id") REFERENCES population_types ("population_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "population_types" (
  "population_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each population type */,
  "population_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the population type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the population type */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the population type */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the population type is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the population type was added to the system */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the population type was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("population_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password (hashed) for the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the user_id in the users table */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the role_id in the roles table */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user-role relationship was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)
